title: 三只瞎老鼠
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\151-三只瞎老鼠-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
After arriving on Majorca Island via steamship from Barcelona, Mr. Parker Pyne finds the local hotels fully booked due to the peak season. After staying in an unsatisfactory room in the city center, he consults a Spanish taxi driver regarding hotel prices in areas like Bunyanyola and Palma. Finding the prices exorbitant, Pyne decides to seek accommodation himself. He eventually settles on the Pino d'Oro, a small seaside inn near Bolinas.

While having breakfast on the terrace, Pyne observes a British mother and her son, Basil Chester. Although the woman appears composed and well-traveled, Pyne notices a sudden, tense stiffness in her back that suggests she is struggling to suppress tears. Sensing his peaceful vacation may be interrupted, Pyne decides to obscure his identity in the hotel register, signing as "Christopher Pyn" instead of "C. Parker Pyne" to avoid being recognized by the Chesters.

Over the following days, Pyne interacts with the Chesters. He finds Basil to be a modest and polite young man, and the three British guests occasionally gather for coffee. On the fourth day, Pyne speaks privately with Mrs. Chester. While they initially discuss mundane topics like gardening and economics, Mrs. Chester eventually begins to share details about Basil’s academic successes and his character, expressing pride in how he has remained well-behaved.
